fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/comments/1825413857","pull_request_review_id":2409448963,"id":1825413857,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAdw-Nc5szZrh","diff_hunk":"@@ -55,6 +57,18 @@
 __all__ = ("KubernetesJobRunner",)
 
 
+@dataclass
+class RetryableDeleteJobState(JobState):
+    def __init__(self, job_state, k8s_job, max_retries=5, attempts=0):
+        self.__dict__ = job_state.__dict__.copy()","path":"lib/galaxy/jobs/runners/kubernetes.py","commit_id":"5ba725022e9437d3364febd634b8f6d291eaed41","original_commit_id":"5ba725022e9437d3364febd634b8f6d291eaed41","user":{"login":"nuwang","id":2070605,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNzA2MDU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2070605?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang","html_url":"https://github.com/nuwang","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"That's an interesting question. I am really not sure. On the one hand, this delete exception was a side-effect of: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19001/files and was at best, a misleading proxy for it. I think the failure would have occurred regardless. However, perhaps it's better to have a misleading proxy that a (mostly) silent failure.

On the other hand, this pattern of retrying is not uncommon in k8s to resolve transient failures, especially since object versions can indeed mismatch due to parallel modifications (e.g. by external, non-galaxy processes). However, since we've had no history of this being an issue other than this case, perhaps we can just let things be until it does become an issue.

Would be great to have your opinions @mapk-amazon and @afgane needed to support refreshing the temporary tokens that were associated with a user's OIDC login via Elixir AAI. We hacked this into place. This issue serves as documentation of the Proof of Concept we have, that we'd love to see translated to a proper implementation. Fyi for @nuwang and maybe also @poterlowicz-lab who I believe is also involved in some way.

## Todos

- [ ] Refreshing tokens should be built into galaxy via celery beat, not a cron script
- [ ] ga4gh passports support is needed (i.e. changes to the auth library).
- [ ] Do we need a better access method inside tools for accessing these tokens? That feels like it should be declared somewhere, like how ITs declare an API key requirement.

## Refreshing

We setup a cron job that runs every 15 minutes, refreshing any user tokens:

```
*/15 * * * * cd /srv/galaxy/server && /srv/galaxy/venv/bin/python ~/refresh.py -c /srv/galaxy/config/galaxy.yml
```

The `refresh.py` script looks like follows

```
import datetime
import time
import requests
import sys

sys.path.insert(1, '/srv/galaxy/server/lib')

from galaxy.model import *  # noqa
from galaxy.model.mapping import init
from galaxy.model.orm.scripts import get_config
from jose import jwt
import jose

config = get_config(sys.argv)
db_url = config['db_url']
sa_session = init('/tmp/', db_url).context

# For all users
users = sa_session.query(User).enable_eagerloads(False).all()
for user in users:
    tokens = user.social_auth

    # For each of their tokens, refresh it IFF it's an elixir token, and we were provided a refresh token.
    for token in tokens:
        ed = token.extra_data
        if token.provider != 'elixir':
            continue
        if 'refresh_token' not in ed:
            continue

        rt = ed['refresh_token']
        now = time.mktime(datetime.utcnow().utctimetuple())

        # The verification here is not simple, but we should be doing this properly instead of accepting it. That said we can generally trust the IdP.
        try:
            at = jwt.decode(ed['access_token'], None, options={"verify_signature": False})
            exp = at['exp']
        except Exception:
            # Just assume expired
            exp = 0

        #print(f"{at['sub']} expires in {exp - now}")
        # If the token is expiring soon, we'll refresh it.
        if exp - now - 600 < 0:
            #print(exp - now - 3580)
            #print(f"Replacing {token}")
            data = {
                'grant_type': 'refresh_token',
                'client_id': 'redacted',
                'client_secret': 'redacted',
                'refresh_token': rt
            }
            r = requests.post('https://login.elixir-czech.org/oidc/token', data=data)
            token.extra_data = r.json()
            print(token)
            print(token.extra_data)
            sa_session.add(token)

sa_session.flush()
```

We additionally need the ga4gh passport fields that are being passed along, but that isn't included in this implementation, and, I believe may require updating social-core to support it.

## Tools

Inside tools I've been doing something like:

```
#for token in $__user__.social_auth:
  #if $token.provider == "elixir":
    #set access_token = $token.extra_data.get('access_token', '')
    "token": "${access_token}",
  #end if
#end for
```

(assuming that there will only ever be a single elixir provider), and passing that along to the tool which uses it for authenticating.

I think I'd rather see something like

```xml
    <environment_variables>
        <environment_variable name="LS_TOKEN" provider="elixir" inject="access_token" />
    </environment_variables>
```

(elixir aai has since been renamed lifescience login or something)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/14578/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/14578/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

IMO you'd probably not want these warnings on your local TPV config, but we probably do want them on the shared discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

IMO you'd probably not want these warnings on your local TPV config, but we probably do want them on the shared discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

IMO you'd probably not want these warnings on your local TPV config, but we probably do want them on the shared luckily, we have all the data to reason about better `mem` values (and `cores` should probably typically be `mem/4` as discussed in #60 except in cases where tools use little memory but see significant speedup with more cores).

If we all pushed our memory usage and input sizes to a centralized database we could both visualize it (similar to how I have done it one-off [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551)) and hopefully automatically make some decisions about memory values in the shared DB.

However there are some things for consideration by people who are good at statistics:

- Cutoffs for high and low memory usage (or just use 95th percentile?) since there are outliers
- Cutoffs for high and low size inputs, since there is usually a lower bound on memory that does not correlate to inputs at all
- Input compression - the mixture of compressed and uncompressed data makes input sizes as a ratio to memory usage kind of a lie
- The current memory limit can arbitrarily cut off what would be valid successful jobs and thus skew the data, and this varies by server, although we do know what the limit was for each job
- How input size affects memory usage, and of course it is rarely just input size, but also the actual data itself
- How recent of jobs to consider, since newer data is typically more useful than older data
- Tool versions to consider, since this can drastically effect memory usage, but we also don't necessarily want each `+galaxyN` version to be separate","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/comments/2458640003","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75#issuecomment-2458640003","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/75","id":2458640003,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHd5Rz86Si96D","user":{"login":"nuwang","id":2070605,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNzA2MDU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2070605?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang","html_url":"https://github.com/nuwang","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-06T03:08:34Z","updated_at":"2024-11-06T03:08:34Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"@natefoo Have you seen this PR? https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/pull/64

discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

IMO you'd probably not want these warnings on your local TPV config, but we probably do want them on the shared discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

IMO you'd probably not want these warnings on your local TPV config, but we probably do want them on the shared discussion in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/73.

~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. ~~I looked a bit at adding `#noqa` support but reading comments and figuring out the line they are associated with seemed a bit tricky. But if it's something we want I could work on that. Alternatively, `tpv lint --ignore=...` to simply ignore an entire class of warnings would be easy to implement, but then it's all-or-nothing.~~
I went ahead and added `# noqa` and command line `tpv lint --ignore=...` support. `# noqa` comments apply to the whole entity they are contained within and can either be without a specific code (in which case all warnings are ignored) or can specify codes as a comma-separated list like `# noqa: T101, T102, ...`. Inline `# noqa` comments are not supported.

IMO you'd probably not want these warnings on your local TPV config, but we probably do want them on the shared https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17511
Work in progress...
I will add a full description when it is done.

Adding secrets to tool requirements like this:
```xml
<requirements>
    <secret type="vault" user_preferences_key="some_tool/api_key" inject_as_env="some_tool_api_key" label="API Key" required="true"/>
</requirements>
```
This will make use of the vault key that has been added in user_preferences_extra:
```yaml
preferences:
  some_tool:
    description: Your Tool settings
    inputs:
      - name: api_key
        label: API Key
        type: secret
        store: vault
        required: False
```
And inject it as an environmental variable that could be accessed within tools.

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17511
Work in progress...
I will add a full description when it is done.

Adding secrets to tool requirements like this:
```xml
<requirements>
    <secret type="vault" user_preferences_key="some_tool/api_key" inject_as_env="some_tool_api_key" label="API Key" required="true"/>
</requirements>
```
This will make use of the vault key that has been added in user_preferences_extra:
```yaml
preferences:
  some_tool:
    description: Your Tool settings
    inputs:
      - name: api_key
        label: API Key
        type: secret
        store: vault
        required: False
```
And inject it as an environmental variable that could be accessed within tools.

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17511
Work in progress...
I will add a full description when it is done.

Adding secrets to tool requirements like this:
```xml
<requirements>
    <secret type="vault" user_preferences_key="some_tool/api_key" inject_as_env="some_tool_api_key" label="API Key" required="true"/>
</requirements>
```
This will make use of the vault key that has been added in user_preferences_extra:
```yaml
preferences:
  some_tool:
    description: Your Tool settings
    inputs:
      - name: api_key
        label: API Key
        type: secret
        store: vault
        required: False
```
And inject it as an environmental variable that could be accessed within tools.

ed_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":64,"closed_issues":174,"state":"open","created_at":"2024-05-14T14:03:11Z","updated_at":"2024-10-15T15:49:45Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-10-15T15:47:50Z","updated_at":"2024-10-16T13:53:10Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/19001","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19001","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19001.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19001.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This fix addresses the random crashes of k8s jobs in Galaxy: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490 and mentioned therein. The issue is that k8s job status may not be ready, while providing already some information: 
https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.30/#jobstatus-v1-batch

In the previous code `if len(job.obj["status"]) == 0:` checked whether some information is there; if yes, treat it as "final" state of the job and continue processing. In the case that the job status has the field `uncountedTerminatedPods`, k8s is not done with analyzing whether the job failed or succeeded. The code then used this information (for me 0 succeeded, 0 active and 0 failed) and went through the decision tree to decide what to do.  

My suggestion is to instead wait for k8s to determine the status of the terminated pods and only then decide what to do. This reduced the failure rate from 2-5% to 0% :)

## How to test the changes?
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The change is difficult to test. It covers an edge where a/ the job is completed, b/ k8s has yet to determine the status of the job. For me 2-5% of jobs fail without the fix.

","user":{"login":"mapk-amazon","id":125464188,"node_id":"U_kgDOB3pufA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/125464188?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon","html_url":"https://github.com/mapk-amazon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This fix addresses the random crashes of k8s jobs in Galaxy: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490 and mentioned therein. The issue is that k8s job status may not be ready, while providing already some information: 
https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.30/#jobstatus-v1-batch

In the previous code `if len(job.obj["status"]) == 0:` checked whether some information is there; if yes, treat it as "final" state of the job and continue processing. In the case that the job status has the field `uncountedTerminatedPods`, k8s is not done with analyzing whether the job failed or succeeded. The code then used this information (for me 0 succeeded, 0 active and 0 failed) and went through the decision tree to decide what to do.  

My suggestion is to instead wait for k8s to determine the status of the terminated pods and only then decide what to do. This reduced the failure rate from 2-5% to 0% :)

## How to test the changes?
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The change is difficult to test. It covers an edge where a/ the job is completed, b/ k8s has yet to determine the status of the job. For me 2-5% of jobs fail without the fix.

Gal…","user":{"login":"Edmontosaurus","id":140177792,"node_id":"U_kgDOCFrxgA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/140177792?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus","html_url":"https://github.com/Edmontosaurus","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds generic OIDC IdP authentication capability to Galaxy and the option to provide more meaningful descriptive texts to IdP sign-in buttons on the Galaxy login page.

* Implemented the generic OIDC backend from python-social-auth. See _https://python-social-auth.readthedocs.io/en/latest/backends/oidc.html_
* This allows Galaxy to use any IdP that supports the "generic" OIDC standard, as opposed to more specific IdPs like Azure or Google.
* The added benefit of including this back-end as one of the IdP authentication options is that any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard can be used for authentication.
* For example: At Naturalis our use-case for needing this psa back-end is to authenticate with our third-party authentication/authorization provider called “SRAM” (See _https://sram.surf.nl/_). This provider supports the OIDC standard which means that with the changes made in this pull-request, it is fairly easy to connect Galaxy to this service (or any IdP that supports OIDC) for authorization.
* Hopefully making this psa back-end available through this pull-request will not only be useful to us at Naturalis, but also maybe to other Galaxy admins that want to connect to an IdP that supports generic OIDC.
* In addition, I’ve also implemented a feature that allows a Galaxy admin to set a custom text for an IdP sign-in button. The added benefit of this is so that one is not tied to the standard button text like “Sign in with Azure” or “Sign in with Oidc” but can for example make the button read “Sign in with your university account”, or whatever one prefers. This custom text can be set by providing your preferred text using the <custom_button_text> element in the **oidc_backends_config.xml** file. When this element is omitted, the default button text will be used.
* This feature is especially useful when providing multiple IdPs for authentication because it is more useful for end-users to differentiate between the available sign-in buttons when they contain more meaningful descriptions.
* I’ve also documented these new features to the **oidc_backends_condig.xml.sample** file to make it easy for other admins to figure out how to configure their generic OIDC IdP for Galaxy or to rename the text of their sign-in buttons.

Feel free to let me know if there are any questions about the above or suggestions for changes to the code that I submitted.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
Any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard and provides an autoconfig file should be able to be configured as an authentication provider for Galaxy by modifying the  oidc_backends_config.xml file. For example:
```
    <provider name="oidc">
        <client_id> [your client_id here] </client_id>
        <client_secret> [your client_secret here] </client_secret>
        <redirect_uri>https://localhost:8080/authnz/oidc/callback</redirect_uri>
        <oidc_endpoint>https://my-idp.example.com</oidc_endpoint>
        <custom_button_text>with your university account</custom_button_text>
    </provider>
```

I tested this myself successfully against both a locally installed Keycloak server and our third-party "SRAM" IdP provider in production which both support the OIDC standard by providing a .well-known/openid-configuration file that Galaxy/python-social-auth can use for auto-configuration.

Gal…","user":{"login":"Edmontosaurus","id":140177792,"node_id":"U_kgDOCFrxgA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/140177792?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus","html_url":"https://github.com/Edmontosaurus","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds generic OIDC IdP authentication capability to Galaxy and the option to provide more meaningful descriptive texts to IdP sign-in buttons on the Galaxy login page.

* Implemented the generic OIDC backend from python-social-auth. See _https://python-social-auth.readthedocs.io/en/latest/backends/oidc.html_
* This allows Galaxy to use any IdP that supports the "generic" OIDC standard, as opposed to more specific IdPs like Azure or Google.
* The added benefit of including this back-end as one of the IdP authentication options is that any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard can be used for authentication.
* For example: At Naturalis our use-case for needing this psa back-end is to authenticate with our third-party authentication/authorization provider called “SRAM” (See _https://sram.surf.nl/_). This provider supports the OIDC standard which means that with the changes made in this pull-request, it is fairly easy to connect Galaxy to this service (or any IdP that supports OIDC) for authorization.
* Hopefully making this psa back-end available through this pull-request will not only be useful to us at Naturalis, but also maybe to other Galaxy admins that want to connect to an IdP that supports generic OIDC.
* In addition, I’ve also implemented a feature that allows a Galaxy admin to set a custom text for an IdP sign-in button. The added benefit of this is so that one is not tied to the standard button text like “Sign in with Azure” or “Sign in with Oidc” but can for example make the button read “Sign in with your university account”, or whatever one prefers. This custom text can be set by providing your preferred text using the <custom_button_text> element in the **oidc_backends_config.xml** file. When this element is omitted, the default button text will be used.
* This feature is especially useful when providing multiple IdPs for authentication because it is more useful for end-users to differentiate between the available sign-in buttons when they contain more meaningful descriptions.
* I’ve also documented these new features to the **oidc_backends_condig.xml.sample** file to make it easy for other admins to figure out how to configure their generic OIDC IdP for Galaxy or to rename the text of their sign-in buttons.

Feel free to let me know if there are any questions about the above or suggestions for changes to the code that I submitted.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
Any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard and provides an autoconfig file should be able to be configured as an authentication provider for Galaxy by modifying the  oidc_backends_config.xml file. For example:
```
    <provider name="oidc">
        <client_id> [your client_id here] </client_id>
        <client_secret> [your client_secret here] </client_secret>
        <redirect_uri>https://localhost:8080/authnz/oidc/callback</redirect_uri>
        <oidc_endpoint>https://my-idp.example.com</oidc_endpoint>
        <custom_button_text>with your university account</custom_button_text>
    </provider>
```

I tested this myself successfully against both a locally installed Keycloak server and our third-party "SRAM" IdP provider in production which both support the OIDC standard by providing a .well-known/openid-configuration file that Galaxy/python-social-auth can use for auto-configuration.

Gal…","user":{"login":"Edmontosaurus","id":140177792,"node_id":"U_kgDOCFrxgA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/140177792?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus","html_url":"https://github.com/Edmontosaurus","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds generic OIDC IdP authentication capability to Galaxy and the option to provide more meaningful descriptive texts to IdP sign-in buttons on the Galaxy login page.

* Implemented the generic OIDC backend from python-social-auth. See _https://python-social-auth.readthedocs.io/en/latest/backends/oidc.html_
* This allows Galaxy to use any IdP that supports the "generic" OIDC standard, as opposed to more specific IdPs like Azure or Google.
* The added benefit of including this back-end as one of the IdP authentication options is that any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard can be used for authentication.
* For example: At Naturalis our use-case for needing this psa back-end is to authenticate with our third-party authentication/authorization provider called “SRAM” (See _https://sram.surf.nl/_). This provider supports the OIDC standard which means that with the changes made in this pull-request, it is fairly easy to connect Galaxy to this service (or any IdP that supports OIDC) for authorization.
* Hopefully making this psa back-end available through this pull-request will not only be useful to us at Naturalis, but also maybe to other Galaxy admins that want to connect to an IdP that supports generic OIDC.
* In addition, I’ve also implemented a feature that allows a Galaxy admin to set a custom text for an IdP sign-in button. The added benefit of this is so that one is not tied to the standard button text like “Sign in with Azure” or “Sign in with Oidc” but can for example make the button read “Sign in with your university account”, or whatever one prefers. This custom text can be set by providing your preferred text using the <custom_button_text> element in the **oidc_backends_config.xml** file. When this element is omitted, the default button text will be used.
* This feature is especially useful when providing multiple IdPs for authentication because it is more useful for end-users to differentiate between the available sign-in buttons when they contain more meaningful descriptions.
* I’ve also documented these new features to the **oidc_backends_condig.xml.sample** file to make it easy for other admins to figure out how to configure their generic OIDC IdP for Galaxy or to rename the text of their sign-in buttons.

Feel free to let me know if there are any questions about the above or suggestions for changes to the code that I submitted.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
Any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard and provides an autoconfig file should be able to be configured as an authentication provider for Galaxy by modifying the  oidc_backends_config.xml file. For example:
```
    <provider name="oidc">
        <client_id> [your client_id here] </client_id>
        <client_secret> [your client_secret here] </client_secret>
        <redirect_uri>https://localhost:8080/authnz/oidc/callback</redirect_uri>
        <oidc_endpoint>https://my-idp.example.com</oidc_endpoint>
        <custom_button_text>with your university account</custom_button_text>
    </provider>
```

I tested this myself successfully against both a locally installed Keycloak server and our third-party "SRAM" IdP provider in production which both support the OIDC standard by providing a .well-known/openid-configuration file that Galaxy/python-social-auth can use for auto-configuration.

For init containers, the same parameters should exists. Currently hard coded to alpine:3.16.

```
      initContainers:
        - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}-wait-postgres
          image: alpine:3.16
```
```
        - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}-init-cloud-repo
          image: alpine:3.16
```
and so on.

the generic OIDC backend from python-social-auth into Gal…","user":{"login":"Edmontosaurus","id":140177792,"node_id":"U_kgDOCFrxgA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/140177792?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus","html_url":"https://github.com/Edmontosaurus","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds generic OIDC IdP authentication capability to Galaxy and the option to provide more meaningful descriptive texts to IdP sign-in buttons on the Galaxy login page.

* Implemented the generic OIDC backend from python-social-auth. See _https://python-social-auth.readthedocs.io/en/latest/backends/oidc.html_
* This allows Galaxy to use any IdP that supports the "generic" OIDC standard, as opposed to more specific IdPs like Azure or Google.
* The added benefit of including this back-end as one of the IdP authentication options is that any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard can be used for authentication.
* For example: At Naturalis our use-case for needing this psa back-end is to authenticate with our third-party authentication/authorization provider called “SRAM” (See _https://sram.surf.nl/_). This provider supports the OIDC standard which means that with the changes made in this pull-request, it is fairly easy to connect Galaxy to this service (or any IdP that supports OIDC) for authorization.
* Hopefully making this psa back-end available through this pull-request will not only be useful to us at Naturalis, but also maybe to other Galaxy admins that want to connect to an IdP that supports generic OIDC.
* In addition, I’ve also implemented a feature that allows a Galaxy admin to set a custom text for an IdP sign-in button. The added benefit of this is so that one is not tied to the standard button text like “Sign in with Azure” or “Sign in with Oidc” but can for example make the button read “Sign in with your university account”, or whatever one prefers. This custom text can be set by providing your preferred text using the <custom_button_text> element in the **oidc_backends_config.xml** file. When this element is omitted, the default button text will be used.
* This feature is especially useful when providing multiple IdPs for authentication because it is more useful for end-users to differentiate between the available sign-in buttons when they contain more meaningful descriptions.
* I’ve also documented these new features to the **oidc_backends_condig.xml.sample** file to make it easy for other admins to figure out how to configure their generic OIDC IdP for Galaxy or to rename the text of their sign-in buttons.

Feel free to let me know if there are any questions about the above or suggestions for changes to the code that I submitted.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
Any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard and provides an autoconfig file should be able to be configured as an authentication provider for Galaxy by modifying the  oidc_backends_config.xml file. For example:
```
    <provider name="oidc">
        <client_id> [your client_id here] </client_id>
        <client_secret> [your client_secret here] </client_secret>
        <redirect_uri>https://localhost:8080/authnz/oidc/callback</redirect_uri>
        <oidc_endpoint>https://my-idp.example.com</oidc_endpoint>
        <custom_button_text>with your university account</custom_button_text>
    </provider>
```

I tested this myself successfully against both a locally installed Keycloak server and our third-party "SRAM" IdP provider in production which both support the OIDC standard by providing a .well-known/openid-configuration file that Galaxy/python-social-auth can use for auto-configuration.

authorization"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/milestones/27","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/milestone/27","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/milestones/27/labels","id":10606334,"node_id":"MI_kwDOAdw-Nc4Aodb-","number":27,"title":"24.1","description":"","creator":{"login":"jdavcs","id":6132258,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxMzIyNTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6132258?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs","html_url":"https://github.com/jdavcs","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":6,"closed_issues":407,"state":"closed","created_at":"2024-02-26T16:02:27Z","updated_at":"2024-09-06T07:35:36Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":"2024-06-20T16:48:56Z"},"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-08-09T12:23:05Z","updated_at":"2024-09-06T19:14:35Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/18670","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18670","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18670.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18670.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Adds generic OIDC IdP authentication capability to Galaxy and the option to provide more meaningful descriptive texts to IdP sign-in buttons on the Galaxy login page.

* Implemented the generic OIDC backend from python-social-auth. See _https://python-social-auth.readthedocs.io/en/latest/backends/oidc.html_
* This allows Galaxy to use any IdP that supports the "generic" OIDC standard, as opposed to more specific IdPs like Azure or Google.
* The added benefit of including this back-end as one of the IdP authentication options is that any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard can be used for authentication.
* For example: At Naturalis our use-case for needing this psa back-end is to authenticate with our third-party authentication/authorization provider called “SRAM” (See _https://sram.surf.nl/_). This provider supports the OIDC standard which means that with the changes made in this pull-request, it is fairly easy to connect Galaxy to this service (or any IdP that supports OIDC) for authorization.
* Hopefully making this psa back-end available through this pull-request will not only be useful to us at Naturalis, but also maybe to other Galaxy admins that want to connect to an IdP that supports generic OIDC.
* In addition, I’ve also implemented a feature that allows a Galaxy admin to set a custom text for an IdP sign-in button. The added benefit of this is so that one is not tied to the standard button text like “Sign in with Azure” or “Sign in with Oidc” but can for example make the button read “Sign in with your university account”, or whatever one prefers. This custom text can be set by providing your preferred text using the <custom_button_text> element in the **oidc_backends_config.xml** file. When this element is omitted, the default button text will be used.
* This feature is especially useful when providing multiple IdPs for authentication because it is more useful for end-users to differentiate between the available sign-in buttons when they contain more meaningful descriptions.
* I’ve also documented these new features to the **oidc_backends_condig.xml.sample** file to make it easy for other admins to figure out how to configure their generic OIDC IdP for Galaxy or to rename the text of their sign-in buttons.

Feel free to let me know if there are any questions about the above or suggestions for changes to the code that I submitted.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
Any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard and provides an autoconfig file should be able to be configured as an authentication provider for Galaxy by modifying the  oidc_backends_config.xml file. For example:
```
    <provider name="oidc">
        <client_id> [your client_id here] </client_id>
        <client_secret> [your client_secret here] </client_secret>
        <redirect_uri>https://localhost:8080/authnz/oidc/callback</redirect_uri>
        <oidc_endpoint>https://my-idp.example.com</oidc_endpoint>
        <custom_button_text>with your university account</custom_button_text>
    </provider>
```

I tested this myself successfully against both a locally installed Keycloak server and our third-party "SRAM" IdP provider in production which both support the OIDC standard by providing a .well-known/openid-configuration file that Galaxy/python-social-auth can use for auto-configuration.

images used can be specified, and overridden, in the `values.yaml` file.  Except the images used in the init containers in the `jobs_init.yaml` template.

This PR parameterizes those images (four in total) as well.  The only issue to be settled is where in the `values.yaml` file these should be specified.  For now I have added them under the `jobs` block:
```
#- Additional dynamic rules to map into the container.
jobs:
  init:
    #- The Docker image to use for the init containers
    image:
      repository: alpine
      tag: 3.16
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
```
Closes #499

container name and tag can be customized for ngix and galaxy, e.g. through `nginx.image.tag` and `nginx.image.repository`. For init containers, the same parameters should exists. Currently hard coded to alpine:3.16.

```
      initContainers:
        - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}-wait-postgres
          image: alpine:3.16
```
```
        - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}-init-cloud-repo
          image: alpine:3.16
```
and so on.

{"action":"closed","number":500,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/pulls/500","id":2058727804,"node_id":"PR_kwDOBCYhWs56tbF8","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/pull/500","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/pull/500.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/pull/500.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/500","number":500,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Parameterize the image used for init containers.","user":{"login":"ksuderman","id":4098072,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwOTgwNzI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4098072?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman","html_url":"https://github.com/ksuderman","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Most images used can be specified, and overridden, in the `values.yaml` file.  Except the images used in the init containers in the `jobs_init.yaml` template.

This PR parameterizes those images (four in total) as well.  The only issue to be settled is where in the `values.yaml` file these should be specified.  For now I have added them under the `jobs` block:
```
#- Additional dynamic rules to map into the container.
jobs:
  init:
    #- The Docker image to use for the init containers
    image:
      repository: alpine
      tag: 3.16
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
```
Closes #499

{"action":"closed","number":18670,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/18670","id":2012302308,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAdw-Nc538Uvk","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18670","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18670.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18670.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18670","number":18670,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Implemented the generic OIDC backend from python-social-auth into Gal…","user":{"login":"Edmontosaurus","id":140177792,"node_id":"U_kgDOCFrxgA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/140177792?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus","html_url":"https://github.com/Edmontosaurus","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds generic OIDC IdP authentication capability to Galaxy and the option to provide more meaningful descriptive texts to IdP sign-in buttons on the Galaxy login page.

* Implemented the generic OIDC backend from python-social-auth. See _https://python-social-auth.readthedocs.io/en/latest/backends/oidc.html_
* This allows Galaxy to use any IdP that supports the "generic" OIDC standard, as opposed to more specific IdPs like Azure or Google.
* The added benefit of including this back-end as one of the IdP authentication options is that any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard can be used for authentication.
* For example: At Naturalis our use-case for needing this psa back-end is to authenticate with our third-party authentication/authorization provider called “SRAM” (See _https://sram.surf.nl/_). This provider supports the OIDC standard which means that with the changes made in this pull-request, it is fairly easy to connect Galaxy to this service (or any IdP that supports OIDC) for authorization.
* Hopefully making this psa back-end available through this pull-request will not only be useful to us at Naturalis, but also maybe to other Galaxy admins that want to connect to an IdP that supports generic OIDC.
* In addition, I’ve also implemented a feature that allows a Galaxy admin to set a custom text for an IdP sign-in button. The added benefit of this is so that one is not tied to the standard button text like “Sign in with Azure” or “Sign in with Oidc” but can for example make the button read “Sign in with your university account”, or whatever one prefers. This custom text can be set by providing your preferred text using the <custom_button_text> element in the **oidc_backends_config.xml** file. When this element is omitted, the default button text will be used.
* This feature is especially useful when providing multiple IdPs for authentication because it is more useful for end-users to differentiate between the available sign-in buttons when they contain more meaningful descriptions.
* I’ve also documented these new features to the **oidc_backends_condig.xml.sample** file to make it easy for other admins to figure out how to configure their generic OIDC IdP for Galaxy or to rename the text of their sign-in buttons.

Feel free to let me know if there are any questions about the above or suggestions for changes to the code that I submitted.


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
Any IdP that supports the generic OIDC standard and provides an autoconfig file should be able to be configured as an authentication provider for Galaxy by modifying the  oidc_backends_config.xml file. For example:
```
    <provider name="oidc">
        <client_id> [your client_id here] </client_id>
        <client_secret> [your client_secret here] </client_secret>
        <redirect_uri>https://localhost:8080/authnz/oidc/callback</redirect_uri>
        <oidc_endpoint>https://my-idp.example.com</oidc_endpoint>
        <custom_button_text>with your university account</custom_button_text>
    </provider>
```

I tested this myself successfully against both a locally installed Keycloak server and our third-party "SRAM" IdP provider in production which both support the OIDC standard by providing a .well-known/openid-configuration file that Galaxy/python-social-auth can use for auto-configuration.

{"repository_id":31211061,"push_id":20140336931,"size":3,"distinct_size":3,"ref":"refs/heads/release_24.1","head":"a7fe1d0aa5f54fdec61fcce055d954bac40bef58","before":"5b4d8a1688de8ce530018c548096f8824b7cd8a1","commits":[{"sha":"976bbab352a6280ca80126ab3cb8dbaeba7aca23","author":{"email":"2013c8b67556e357801e7f3dce92f10d8b59f692@users.noreply.gitlab.com","name":"Edwin"},"message":"Implemented the generic OIDC backend from python-social-auth into Galaxy.

* Implemented the generic OIDC backend from python-social-auth. See https://python-social-auth.readthedocs.io/en/latest/backends/oidc.html
* This allows Galaxy to use any IdP that supports the "generic" OIDC standard, as opposed to more specific IdPs like Azure or Google.
* By default, configuring galaxy to use the generic OIDC backend will display the "Login with Oidc" text on the sign-in button.
* From an end-user perspective this may be confusing since it isn't exactly clear what IdP is actually being used. So I've added a new "<custom_button_text>" option in the backend configuration that allows for configuring a custom text for the sign-in button.
* So for example, the default "Sign in with Oidc" button text can be changed into "Sign in with your university account" or whatever else you want to display there.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/commits/976bbab352a6280ca80126ab3cb8dbaeba7aca23"},{"sha":"d3a87e2547f9c0bb932dc23ae295739e8096eefa","author":{"email":"2013c8b67556e357801e7f3dce92f10d8b59f692@users.noreply.gitlab.com","name":"Edwin den Haas"},"message":"Fixed some linting mistakes.

Notification function","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-09-12T14:13:42Z","updated_at":"2024-09-12T14:25:13Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We discussed about how to inform users that their ITs will be terminated after 24h. There were several suggestions: The website, training material, as static text in the 'running ITs' window and a notification that is send to users.
So I came to the idea that maybe TPV can send notifications to the user via the Galaxy API. These could be specified e.g. similar to the `fail` message and be triggered by a rule like:
~~~
- if: input_size > 10
  notify:
    msg: 
    subject: "Tool suggestion for *small file Tool*",
    message: "Please consider using the *tool for larger input files*",
    category: "info"
# OR
- if: true
  notify:
    subject: "24h lifetime for Interactive Tools",
    message: "For giving everyone a shorter waiting time we terminate Interactive tools after 24h",
    category: "warning"

~~~

Please share your opinions and ideas","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/139/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/139/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/comments/2346454296","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/139#issuecomment-2346454296","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/139","id":2346454296,"node_id":"IC_kwDOFe5uac6L3A0Y","user":{"login":"nuwang","id":2070605,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNzA2MDU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2070605?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang","html_url":"https://github.com/nuwang","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-12T14:25:12Z","updated_at":"2024-09-12T14:25:12Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"If this is used often enough, providing a shorthand syntax may be worthwhile, but I'll wait for others to chime in. a group through admin UI can lead to duplication of user_group_association entries","user":{"login":"cat-bro","id":22670687,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyNjcwNjg3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22670687?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro","html_url":"https://github.com/cat-bro","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":317005221,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzMTcwMDUyMjE=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/database","name":"area/database","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":"Galaxy's database or data access layer"},{"id":3910035348,"node_id":"LA_kwDOAdw-Nc7pDmeU","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/backend","name":"area/backend","color":"006B75","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-08-02T02:01:37Z","updated_at":"2024-08-06T06:30:40Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
The number of user_group_assocation entries for Galaxy Australia’s most populous user group increased from 3,000 to 19,000 in the space of 10 minutes. In the nginx logs there were 6 requests submitted within 3 minutes of `PUT /admin/manage_users_and_roles_for_group?id=2b32d97164233881` (the same group ID) and all of these requests timed out.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Australia, release_24.1
`3be367228b531c346c10700f07d57ae44394be36`

**To Reproduce**
As admin
1) Add 1000 members to a user group. (Admin -> Groups -> <Group Name> -> Edit Permissions) and save group.
2) Add another user
3) Click Save a few times - the button is not disabled
4) Check db for multiple user_group_association entries `select user_id, group_id, count(id) from user_group_association where group_id = <group_id> group by user_id, group_id having count(id) > 1;`

**Expected behavior**
If a user_group_association exists for user X, group Y another one will not be created.

","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18636/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18636/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/2270485988","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18636#issuecomment-2270485988","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18636","id":2270485988,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAdw-Nc6HVN3k","user":{"login":"nuwang","id":2070605,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNzA2MDU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2070605?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang","html_url":"https://github.com/nuwang","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-06T06:30:39Z","updated_at":"2024-08-06T06:30:39Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"There are several related problems that seem to be causing this:
1. The database does not have a unique constraint on the (user_id, group_id) combination
2. When a number of users are added/removed from a group [here](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/7b34535e0bd4c73e3f93b57db8427719e4889914/lib/galaxy/model/security.py#L717), it's not an atomic operation. This means that two operations modifying the same group result in an indeterminate outcome. (And currently, the lack of a unique constraint means that the same user could be associated multiple times with the same group).
3. The same issue is probably present for [`associate_user_role`](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/7b34535e0bd4c73e3f93b57db8427719e4889914/lib/galaxy/model/security.py#L724), [`associate_group_role`](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/7b34535e0bd4c73e3f93b57db8427719e4889914/lib/galaxy/model/security.py#L731) and [`associate_action_dataset_role`](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/7b34535e0bd4c73e3f93b57db8427719e4889914/lib/galaxy/model/security.py#L738).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/2270485988/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

The setup is deployed on AWS on EKS: 
- Version k8s: 1.28
- Version Helm Chart: v5.9.0

## Issue 

Galaxy "usually" deploys jobs just fine. We started importing with Batch files into Galaxy and experience random failures of pods. 

## Logs

```

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/492","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/492/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/492/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/492/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/492","id":2448870435,"node_id":"I_kwDOBCYhWs6R9swj","number":492,"title":"Need Guidance on S3FS / CVMFS ","user":{"login":"NilsKrattinger","id":45068998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ1MDY4OTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/45068998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/NilsKrattinger","html_url":"https://github.com/NilsKrattinger","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NilsKrattinger/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NilsKrattinger/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NilsKrattinger/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NilsKrattinger/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NilsKrattinger/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NilsKrattinger/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NilsKrattinger/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NilsKrattinger/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NilsKrattinger/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-08-05T15:38:56Z","updated_at":"2024-08-06T16:31:25Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi, 

I'm currently working on the installation of Galaxy from the helm chart and, I have come across some questions concerning the CVMFS / S3FS choice for the `ref-data` volume.

The fact that in multiple places CVMFS is listed as the default choice for "time being", and this [answer](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/342#issuecomment-942075949) on the issue [#342](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/342) are making me wonder if you have plan to drop on CVMFS support ?

But on the other hand, the [CSI-S3](https://github.com/ctrox/csi-s3) project seems to not be maintained anymore ? Is it planned to switch to the Yandex [k8s-csi-s3](https://github.com/yandex-cloud/k8s-csi-s3) alternative ? 

Is there any long-term plan discussed on this topic ? What would be the most future-proof choice ?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/492/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/492/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/comments/2271692831","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/492#issuecomment-2271692831","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/492","id":2271692831,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBCYhWs6HZ0gf","user":{"login":"nuwang","id":2070605,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNzA2MDU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2070605?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang","html_url":"https://github.com/nuwang","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-06T16:31:24Z","updated_at":"2024-08-06T16:31:24Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"Hi @NilsKrattinger We would recommend continuing to use CVMFS, for several reasons.
1. In general, cvmfs has been the main reference data source used by the galaxy community.
2. We considered alternatives because there was a period in which cvmfs-csi lacked maintenance. This has been resolved now, and we are seeing regular updates.
3. s3-csi (and alternatives like Yandex), do not perform as well as cvmfs when dealing with lots of small files. This is because each file requires a request, whereas cvmfs uses a larger block based format.
4. The above + the fact that cvmfs is generally optimized for read-only data, and the s3 alternatives are not, resulted in worse performance for refdata. We observed a dramatic increase in startup times after switching to it.

For these reasons, we have switched back to cvmfs as the refdata repository of choice. Ideally however, the choice should not matter, as it should (at least in principle) be trivially possible to switch from one to the other just by enabling/disabling the corresponding option, and the chart will just use the new source.

Needs manual vetting.

This repository contains the code for generating automated updates: https://github.com/nuwang/tpv-db-optimizer
Once we refine the details, we can transfer the repo over to the galaxyproject.

These scripts rely on the existence of some materialized views in the usegalaxy.* databases, which are defined here:
https://github.com/nuwang/tpv-db-optimizer/blob/0cc2ba687b709120295476fd7da1f1500a551bdd/views.py#L2

Therefore, the script is currently run on a consolidated database which was manually assembled. Instead, it would probably be better if we could run it directly against usegalaxy.* databases, assuming the materialized views exist and are periodically updated.

Needs manual vetting.

This repository contains the code for generating automated updates: https://github.com/nuwang/tpv-db-optimizer
Once we refine the details, we can transfer the repo over to the galaxyproject.

These scripts rely on the existence of some materialized views in the usegalaxy.* databases, which are defined here:
https://github.com/nuwang/tpv-db-optimizer/blob/0cc2ba687b709120295476fd7da1f1500a551bdd/views.py#L2

Therefore, the script is currently run on a consolidated database which was manually assembled. Instead, it would probably be better if we could run it directly against usegalaxy.* databases, assuming the materialized views exist and are periodically updated.

These views could potentially be used for gxy.io/KUI as historical data.

Needs manual vetting.

This repository contains the code for generating automated updates: https://github.com/nuwang/tpv-db-optimizer
Once we refine the details, we can transfer the repo over to the galaxyproject.

These scripts rely on the existence of some materialized views in the usegalaxy.* databases, which are defined here:
https://github.com/nuwang/tpv-db-optimizer/blob/0cc2ba687b709120295476fd7da1f1500a551bdd/views.py#L2

Therefore, the script is currently run on a consolidated database which was manually assembled. Instead, it would probably be better if we could run it directly against usegalaxy.* databases, assuming the materialized views exist and are periodically updated.

These views could potentially be used for gxy.io/KUI as well.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/64/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/64/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/comments/2276393011","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/pull/64#issuecomment-2276393011","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/64","id":2276393011,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHd5Rz86HrwAz","user":{"login":"nuwang","id":2070605,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNzA2MDU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2070605?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang","html_url":"https://github.com/nuwang","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nuwang/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-08T18:11:16Z","updated_at":"2024-08-08T18:11:16Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"Thanks @sanjaysrikakulam and @bgruening for reviewing. Currently, the memory usage is calculated based on the max recorded cgroup memory usage across the [past year](https://github.com/nuwang/tpv-db-optimizer/blob/0cc2ba687b709120295476fd7da1f1500a551bdd/mem-optimize.py#L54). Only one year was used because older cgroup metrics are quite iffy - older ones seem to capture the entire node etc. etc. yielding incorrect results.

The ones that have dramatically lower max values than recorded do need further investigation, but at least in my previous spot checks, were mostly correct at last as far as the cgroup metrics were concerned. I will check the ones you have flagged in greater detail and report back.

I think there's still a fair bit more work to do to make sure we are recording correct metrics across the entire federation for cpu, memory and if possible, perhaps even gpu. We should also merge this PR soon so we can more easily track actual tpv mem allocations: https://github.com/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/pull/63/files
If we can get these issues fixed, we should be able to enhance the scripts for CPU optimization as well I think, and rerun say mid to late next year with even more data.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/comments/2276393011/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
